---
title: "codon_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d5 — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall global_occupancy — occupancy stats"
date: 2026-06-04
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [22, 8, 18, 17, 17, 26]  # aa-col-added
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![Three-panel bar plot, A-site / P-site / E-site left to right, of per-codon log2 fold-change (median day_10 / median day_5) in whole-transcriptome occupancy. Within each panel the codons are sorted by log2 fold-change high to low; each bar is one codon, coloured by amino-acid class (acidic, basic, hydrophobic, neutral, polar) with the in-frame stop codon in grey (see legend). Y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -1.3 to +0.4; bars above zero are day_10-enriched, below zero are day_10-depleted.](../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d5/codon/composite/APE_codon_Day10_vs_Day5_barplot_composite.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each panel ranks the codons by signed log2 fold-change at one ribosome site (A, P, E): a bar above zero means more day_10 occupancy than day_5 at that site, below zero means less. No significance markers are drawn on the bars.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `codon_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d5.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d5.csv`
  - `aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d0.csv`
  - `codon_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d0.csv`
  - `aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d5_vs_d0.csv`
  - `codon_wilcoxon_timepoint_d5_vs_d0.csv`
- **Test:** Mann-Whitney U (two-sample Wilcoxon rank-sum), day_10 vs day_5
- **Design:** n=4 day_10 vs n=4 day_5 (each n=4 arm pools 2 BWM + 2 control replicates within the timepoint); two-sided Mann-Whitney U on per-(site, codon) occupancy; BH-FDR per A/P/E site (each site is its own family of ~62 codon tests); effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median_day_10 / median_day_5).

### Family-level caveats

- **mw-floor-blocking** — the Mann-Whitney two-sided p-floor for n=4 vs n=4 is exactly 0.02857; with per-site BH families of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon), clearing FDR<0.05 is mathematically near-impossible unless ~every test ties at the floor.
- **condition-pooled-confound** — n=4 per timepoint is built by pooling 2 BWM + 2 control replicates (`rep_to_timepoint`); if BWM and control diverge in their time response, pooling masks divergent signals. All 6 members share this design.
- **n=4-low-power** — n=4 vs n=4 Mann-Whitney has very low power; nulls across all 6 files are weakly informative.
- **p-floor-aware-headline** — Headlines must explicitly state min raw p ~= 0.0286 and explain why "no FDR hits" here is uninformative, not a biological negative.

---

## Headline

0 of 186 day_10-vs-day_5 codon tests are significant at FDR<0.05; minimum adjusted p is 0.9984. No cell reaches even raw p<0.05 (min raw p = 0.0571): this is the flattest file in the family — not a single cell achieves the n=4-vs-n=4 perfect-rank-separation floor (0.02857). It is a coordinated null with no nominally-significant cells. As leads: the largest raw swings overall are the in-frame stop codon TGA (P-site `log2_FC` -1.2608, A-site -1.2306, E-site -0.8135), which are unstable low-support cells (see caveats); among sense codons the largest cells are P-site CCC (+0.3614) and A-site GGG (+0.3535), all with raw p >= 0.057.

## Top hits

Effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median day_10 / median day_5): positive means day_10-enriched, negative means day_10-depleted. Two p-value columns are shown: `p_value` is the raw Mann-Whitney p, and `p_adj` is `p_value` after BH correction within each site's family of ~62 codons (min raw p = 0.0571, the only file in the family whose smallest raw p sits above the n=4 floor of 0.02857). The `aa` column is the single-letter amino acid translation of each codon (the in-frame stop codon shows as `*`). Each site is split into one sub-table per direction. No row reaches FDR<0.05 and no cell reaches raw p<0.05, so every row below is a non-significant lead; within each sub-table the rows are up to 5 per direction ranked by raw `p_value` ascending, with `|log2_FC|` descending as the tiebreaker (since no cell reaches raw p<0.05, the per-direction ranking is mostly carried by the `|log2_FC|` tiebreak). The in-frame stop codon TGA is annotated inline where it ranks into a sub-table (A-site and E-site Depleted); it is a low-support, unstable cell whose large `log2_FC` swings reflect very low footprint support, not a day_10-vs-day_5 effect — see the caveats.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| codon | aa | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---   | ---| ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| GGG   | G  | +0.3535            | 0.2000            | 1.0000               |      |
| CCG   | P  | +0.2255            | 0.2000            | 1.0000               |      |
| CGG   | R  | +0.3436            | 0.3429            | 1.0000               |      |
| GGC   | G  | +0.3001            | 0.3429            | 1.0000               |      |
| TCG   | S  | +0.2491            | 0.3429            | 1.0000               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| codon | aa | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---   | ---| ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| CAA   | Q  | -0.1101            | 0.3429            | 1.0000               |      |
| TGA (in-frame stop; unstable) | * | -1.2306 | 0.4857 | 1.0000 |      |
| AAC   | N  | -0.0786            | 0.6857            | 1.0000               |      |
| AAG   | K  | -0.0173            | 0.6857            | 1.0000               |      |
| GTT   | V  | -0.0295            | 0.8857            | 1.0000               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| codon | aa | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---   | ---| ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| AGG   | R  | +0.2967            | 0.0571            | 0.9984               |      |
| GGG   | G  | +0.2902            | 0.1143            | 0.9984               |      |
| GCA   | A  | +0.2267            | 0.1143            | 0.9984               |      |
| CGG   | R  | +0.3039            | 0.2000            | 0.9984               |      |
| CCT   | P  | +0.2760            | 0.2000            | 0.9984               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| codon | aa | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---   | ---| ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| CTT   | L  | -0.1592            | 0.3429            | 0.9984               |      |
| ATC   | I  | -0.0641            | 0.4857            | 0.9984               |      |
| TTG   | L  | -0.1027            | 0.6857            | 0.9984               |      |
| ATG   | M  | -0.0986            | 0.6857            | 0.9984               |      |
| ATA   | I  | -0.0640            | 0.6857            | 0.9984               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| codon | aa | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---   | ---| ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| CCC   | P  | +0.0998            | 0.2000            | 1.0000               |      |
| CTC   | L  | +0.2516            | 0.3429            | 1.0000               |      |
| CCG   | P  | +0.2393            | 0.3429            | 1.0000               |      |
| CCT   | P  | +0.2950            | 0.4857            | 1.0000               |      |
| AGT   | S  | +0.1335            | 0.4857            | 1.0000               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| codon | aa | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---   | ---| ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| GAG   | E  | -0.1997            | 0.6857            | 1.0000               |      |
| ATT   | I  | -0.0618            | 0.6857            | 1.0000               |      |
| TAT   | Y  | -0.0239            | 0.6857            | 1.0000               |      |
| TTG   | L  | -0.0218            | 0.6857            | 1.0000               |      |
| TGA (in-frame stop; unstable) | * | -0.8135 | 0.8857 | 1.0000 |      |

### Flag glossary

No `flag`-column value appears in any sub-table above: no cell reaches raw p<0.05, so there are no `nominal-only` or `floor` rows. The only annotation is inline on the in-frame stop codon **TGA** (`TGA (in-frame stop; unstable)`), marking the single in-frame stop codon whose large `log2_FC` reflects very low footprint support rather than a day_10-vs-day_5 effect; see the caveats section.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 186 (62 per site, A/P/E; 62 = 61 sense codons + the in-frame stop TGA)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 0
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 0
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.9984 (site P; sites A and E are 1.0000)
- `min raw p`: 0.0571 — the only family member whose minimum raw p sits *above* the n=4 floor (0.02857). No cell achieves perfect 4-vs-4 rank separation.
- `p_floor`: 0.02857 (theoretical two-sided Mann-Whitney floor for n=4 vs n=4 = 2/C(8,4)); not reached in this file.
- Per site: site A min `p_adj` 1.0000; site P min `p_adj` 0.9984; site E min `p_adj` 1.0000.

## Biological interpretation

**There is no detectable day_10-vs-day_5 difference at codon resolution, and the absence is even more clearly structural than in the sister contrasts (concrete).** None of the 186 (site, codon) tests clears BH within its ~62-codon site family; the minimum adjusted p is 0.9984 and the two off-floor sites (A, E) sit at `p_adj` 1.0000. What makes this the flattest file in the family is that no cell reaches even the raw-p floor: the smallest raw p is 0.0571, above the achievable n=4-vs-n=4 two-sided Mann-Whitney minimum of 0.02857. In the other contrasts the FDR null was floor-blocked (cells tied at the floor but the BH family was too wide to clear); here there is not even a floor cell to block, so "0 of 186" reflects an absence of any day_10-vs-day_5 difference at this resolution and N, not a ceiling. This matches the amino-acid sister `aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d5.csv`, also the flattest contrast at its resolution.

**The largest sense-codon swings are directional only and split across residue classes (exploratory).** The largest-magnitude sense cell overall is proline P-site CCC (+0.3614, which the raw-p-ranked sub-tables do not display), followed by glycine A-site GGG (+0.3535) and arginine A-site CGG (+0.3436) — the per-direction tables are ordered by raw `p_value`, not magnitude, so the displayed leads are not the largest cells. Among those displayed leads, glycine and arginine codons (GGG, CGG) recur near the top of both the A-site and P-site enriched tables, and the E-site enriched cells are proline-heavy (CCT +0.2950, CCG +0.2393, CCC +0.0998). None of these cells reaches even raw p<0.05 (`p_adj` 0.9984-1.0000), so these are directions to look for again, not findings — at n=4 the file cannot distinguish them from sampling scatter. They are worth carrying into the within-condition Fisher contrast for the same time axis, where whole-transcriptome N can resolve whether any residue-class lean is real.

**The in-frame stop codon TGA is noise, not signal (concrete).** TGA carries the file's largest raw swings (P-site `log2_FC` -1.2608, A-site -1.2306, E-site -0.8135) but its footprint support is very low: the codon counting path retains in-frame stop windows that the amino-acid path drops (hence 186 codon tests vs the sister's 60), and a handful of readthrough/recoding events drive an unstable magnitude. TGA is non-significant at every site (`p_adj` 1.0000) and appears as a large grey depleted bar in every panel of the plots; read those bars as a low-count artefact, not biology.

**What to carry forward.** Concrete claim to quote: this file shows no signal at FDR<0.05 for day_10-vs-day_5 codon occupancy with conditions pooled, and unlike its sister contrasts the absence is not even floor-blocked — no cell reaches raw p<0.05 (min raw p 0.0571), so it is the flattest file in the family at either resolution. Exploratory patterns worth checking in sister tests: (1) does the A-site + P-site co-enrichment of GGG (glycine) and CGG (arginine) reproduce in the within-condition Fisher `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon`, which holds condition fixed at whole-transcriptome N? (2) does the E-site proline lean (CCT/CCG/CCC) reproduce there, or collapse when synonyms are read separately? Noise to ignore: the in-frame stop codon TGA (low-count), and the bulk of cells sitting at `p_adj` 0.998-1.0 with magnitude but no raw-p support.

## Methods

Columns present: `site`, `codon`, `median_day_10`, `median_day_5`, `log2_FC`, `U_stat`, `p_value`, `p_adj`. The test is a two-sample Wilcoxon rank-sum (Mann-Whitney U) comparing day_10 vs day_5 occupancy per (site, codon) at n=4 vs n=4. The `U_stat` column and the paired median columns are consistent with that test. The correction column is `p_adj` (BH within each site's ~62-codon family, per `merge_global_occupancy_analysis.py`). The test compares the two timepoint distributions per cell; here no cell even reaches the discreteness floor, so the file carries no nominal signal at all.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **mw-floor-blocking.** The n=4-vs-n=4 two-sided Mann-Whitney floor is 0.02857; in this file not even one cell reaches the floor (min raw p 0.0571), so unlike its sister contrasts the floor is not the binding constraint here — the data are simply flat, and "0 of 186 at FDR<0.05" reflects an absence of any day-axis difference at this N, not a structural floor ceiling.
- **condition-pooled-confound.** Each n=4 arm pools 2 BWM + 2 control replicates; a BWM-specific and a control-specific day_5-to-day_10 response that point in opposite directions cancel inside the same comparison, so cross-check the within-condition Fisher (`timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon`) before reading any cell as "no day-axis change."
- **n=4-low-power.** With n=4 vs n=4, a real effect can be missed; treat this no-signal result as weakly informative, not strong evidence that day_10 and day_5 occupancy are equal.
- **p-floor-aware-headline.** The headline reports min raw p = 0.0571 and flags that this is the only family member whose smallest raw p sits above the achievable floor (0.02857); read the 0 of 186 accordingly.
- **stop-codon-instability.** The 62 codons per site are 61 sense codons plus the single in-frame stop TGA (the codon counting path retains stop windows, unlike the amino-acid path, which is why this file has 186 tests vs the amino-acid sister's 60); TGA's footprint support is very low, so its large `log2_FC` swings (P-site -1.2608, A-site -1.2306, E-site -0.8135) are unstable noise rather than a day_10-vs-day_5 effect, and all three are non-significant (`p_adj` 1.0000).

### Considered and ruled out

- **asymptotic-with-ties.** Concern that scipy's Mann-Whitney could fall back to the asymptotic Z approximation under tied per-replicate occupancy values, shifting raw p away from the exact discrete bound. Ruled out empirically. For this file directly: the 186 (site, codon) tests collapse to just 8 distinct p-values, every `U_stat` is integer-valued (range 4 to 15), and all 8 distinct p-values fall exactly on the n=4-vs-n=4 exact two-sided Mann-Whitney grid (k/70 for integer k; here 4/70, 8/70, 14/70, 24/70, 34/70, 48/70, 62/70, 70/70) -- the signature of the exact branch with no ties. The identical n=4-vs-n=4 design was also audited directly on the per-replicate sister data (day_10 vs day_5): 2 of 183 tests carry rank ties (both rare codons, both far from raw p<0.05), `method='auto'` selected the exact branch for 181 tests and the asymptotic branch for those 2, recomputed p matches the pipeline to ~=1e-16 (max 8.3e-17), and forcing `method='asymptotic'` for all tests shifts raw p by at most 0.0759 (median 0.0061), flips zero raw-p<0.05 decisions, and leaves the per-site BH conclusion unchanged (0 hits at FDR<0.05 on either branch). The rule-out carries to this whole-transcriptome occupancy file.

---

## Plots — individual

![A-site day_10-vs-day_5 codon bar plot. X-axis is the codons ordered by log2 fold-change high to low; y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -1.3 to +0.4; bars coloured by amino-acid class with the in-frame stop codon in grey (see legend). Largest enrichment at GGG (~+0.35), with CGG and GGC just behind; the in-frame stop TGA dominates the depleted tail (~-1.23, grey); sense-codon depletions are shallow (largest at CAA / AAC, ~-0.08 to -0.11).](../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d5/codon/individual/site_A_codon_Day10_vs_Day5_barplot.png)

![P-site day_10-vs-day_5 codon bar plot. X-axis is the codons ordered by log2 fold-change high to low; y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -1.3 to +0.4; bars coloured by amino-acid class with the in-frame stop codon in grey. Largest enrichment at CCC (~+0.36), with CCG, CGG, and AGG behind; the in-frame stop TGA dominates the depleted tail (~-1.26, grey) and is by far the largest swing in this panel; the largest sense depletion (CTT) is shallow (~-0.16).](../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d5/codon/individual/site_P_codon_Day10_vs_Day5_barplot.png)

![E-site day_10-vs-day_5 codon bar plot. X-axis is the codons ordered by log2 fold-change high to low; y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.9 to +0.3; bars coloured by amino-acid class with the in-frame stop codon in grey. Largest enrichment at CCT (~+0.30), with CTC and CCG behind; the in-frame stop TGA dominates the depleted tail (~-0.81, grey); the largest sense depletion is GAG (~-0.20).](../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d5/codon/individual/site_E_codon_Day10_vs_Day5_barplot.png)
